projects.title: SCDOT OSHA Reporting Automation
projects.problem: Weekly OSHA reporting required manual extraction and submission across systems, creating long turnaround and human-error risk.
projects.solution: Built an automation pipeline from SQL Server data to the ITA API with validation and failure handling for production-style operational use.
projects.architecture: Scheduled extraction jobs pulled normalized OSHA records from SQL Server, validated required fields, and submitted payloads through the ITA API with retry-safe request handling.
projects.tradeoffs: Prioritized deterministic submission and auditability over UI complexity by shipping backend-first automation before adding operator-facing enhancements.
impact: Reduced reporting turnaround from roughly one week to seconds.
Eliminated repetitive manual entry for internal staff workflows.
Improved consistency of recurring compliance submissions.

projects.title: IMSEL Multi-Camera Sync Pipeline
projects.problem: Four camera streams and IMU processing needed synchronized capture to avoid timing drift and invalid downstream analysis.
projects.solution: Implemented synchronization and control logic with a Qt-based operator interface for stable collection and reduced capture fragility.
projects.architecture: Coordinated acquisition across camera inputs and IMU processing through a unified control path, then exposed operational controls and live state in a Qt desktop interface.
projects.tradeoffs: Focused on synchronization correctness and operator clarity before extending feature breadth to keep data-collection reliability high.
impact: Synchronized four camera streams into one coherent capture workflow.
Reduced operator friction with live-feed visibility and simplified controls.
Improved collection stability for research data workflows.
metrics: Synchronized inputs: 4 camera streams + IMU pipeline
Interface scope: unified control surface for capture operations

projects.title: DermaLite
projects.problem: Skin lesion classification workflows often require heavyweight models that are difficult to run in constrained environments.
projects.solution: Contributed to a lightweight, quantized deep-learning system with explainable visualizations for mobile-friendly classification workflows.
projects.architecture: Used model optimization and explainability outputs in a Python-based ML workflow to support constrained-device inference and interpretation.
projects.tradeoffs: Balanced model compactness and inference practicality against absolute benchmark chasing, prioritizing deployability and interpretability.
impact: Delivered a practical ML system oriented toward constrained-device usage.
Integrated explainability outputs to improve model interpretation.
Shipped in a team setting with active repository collaboration.

projects.title: GradingAgent
projects.problem: Manual design-pattern grading creates inconsistent feedback and slow turnaround in programming courses.
projects.solution: Built an agent-driven grading workflow with companion API/frontend components to structure evaluation and feedback generation.
projects.architecture: Split responsibilities between a grading orchestration layer, API surface, and UI clients to keep rubric logic isolated from delivery channels.
projects.tradeoffs: Accepted smaller initial model scope to establish consistent rubric enforcement and composable service boundaries first.
impact: Established a repeatable grading workflow for pattern-focused assignments.
Separated concerns across core agent, API service, and UI components.
Created a base for iterating on quality and rubric enforcement.
metrics: System shape: core agent + API + frontend components

projects.title: JAIRE x Synchro.io
projects.problem: Athletes and coaches manage nutrition, training, and health data across disconnected tools.
projects.solution: Co-developed an integrated mobile-first experience to unify planning, tracking, and coaching workflows.
projects.architecture: Structured app flows around shared athlete and coach contexts so nutrition, workout, and health states could be viewed and updated in one product surface.
projects.tradeoffs: Optimized for user workflow coherence and feature integration over early breadth to avoid fragmented tool behavior.
impact: Focused product scope around daily coach-athlete workflow friction.
Translated requirements into a more consolidated user experience.
Applied iterative team delivery under real client expectations.
metrics: Workflow domains: nutrition, workouts, health tracking
